untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of 1.0 standard deviation move from 10-period mean and dynamic pyramiding on each new extreme in direction. Standard deviation calculation and pyramiding logic cannot be expressed with allowed indicators and operators. The signal also requires recognition of 'positive feedback loops' and 'amplified expectation shifts' which are behavioral constructs not directly measurable with provided technical indicators. Approximated entry as volatility breakout (true_range > atr_14) with context-dependent direction, but this loses the core feedback-loop and pyramiding mechanics.
translation_notes: ARONSON_DRY_0_2 is a behavioral/feedback-based signal. Approximation uses volatility expansion as proxy for 'significant price move' and exit on 2 consecutive closes against position. Pyramid-on-each-new-high parameter cannot be implemented. True signal intent requires custom volatility normalization and position-sizing logic outside backtest engine scope.
